description: Road Rush MVP - Minimalist arcade vertical racing game inspired by Road Fighter. Core loop: drive, dodge traffic, collect fuel, die, retry instantly.

userStories.title: Project setup with Vite and Canvas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with Vite + vanilla JS, an HTML5 Canvas at 400x700 logical resolution with letterbox scaling, and a basic game loop using requestAnimationFrame with fixed timestep accumulator (1/60s).
acceptanceCriteria: Vite project initialized with vanilla JS (npm create vite@latest -- --template vanilla)
index.html contains a canvas element, main.js initializes it
Canvas logical resolution is 400x700 pixels
Canvas scales to fill viewport while preserving aspect ratio (letterbox with black bars)
Game loop runs with requestAnimationFrame and fixed timestep accumulator at 1/60s
Canvas clears and redraws each frame (verify with a simple colored rectangle moving)
npm run dev starts the dev server successfully
npm run build produces a working production build
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. All game values use logical pixels (400x700). Scaling is purely visual via canvas.style width/height.

userStories.title: Finite State Machine (Title, Playing, GameOver)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a simple FSM to manage game states so that transitions between title screen, gameplay, and game over are clean.
acceptanceCriteria: FSM with 3 states: Title, Playing, GameOver
Title state: renders 'Road Rush' centered text and 'Press Enter to Start' pulsing text (opacity oscillates)
Playing state: entered on Enter key from Title (placeholder: just show a colored background)
GameOver state: shows 'GAME OVER' text and 'Press Enter to Retry'
Enter key in GameOver transitions back to Playing with full state reset
Each state has its own update(dt) and render(ctx) functions
Typecheck passes (no errors in console)
userStories.notes: Keep it simple: object with currentState and transition() method. States are plain objects with update/render/onEnter/onExit.

userStories.title: Road rendering with scrolling lanes
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see an infinite road scrolling downward with lane markers so that I feel the sensation of driving.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark asphalt background with vertical gradient (#1A1A2E top to #16213E bottom)
Road area is 320px wide, centered (40px margin each side)
4 lanes, each 80px wide
3 white dashed lane markers (40% opacity) between lanes, scrolling downward
Solid white border lines on left and right road edges
Red/white alternating rumble strips in the 40px margins on each side, scrolling
Scroll speed starts at 200 px/s and is stored in a game state variable for later modification
Lane markers and rumble strips wrap seamlessly (infinite scroll illusion)
Verify in browser: road looks correct and scrolls smoothly
userStories.notes: Use modular offset for scroll wrapping. Road constants: ROAD_LEFT=40, ROAD_RIGHT=360, ROAD_WIDTH=320, LANE_WIDTH=80, LANE_COUNT=4.

userStories.title: Player car rendering and keyboard movement
userStories.description: As a player, I want to control a car with arrow keys that moves left and right on the road so that I can dodge obstacles.
acceptanceCriteria: Player car: red rounded rectangle (#E53935), 40x64 px
Car positioned at Y=560 (bottom 20% of screen)
Arrow Left/Right moves car horizontally with progressive acceleration: 1200 px/s²
Deceleration when no key pressed: 1800 px/s² (quick stop)
Max lateral speed: 400 px/s
Car constrained within road borders (left edge X >= 40, right edge X <= 360)
Car has a hitbox property at 80% of sprite size (32x51 px, centered within sprite)
Movement feels responsive and smooth at 60 FPS
Verify in browser: car moves smoothly, stops quickly, cannot leave road
userStories.notes: Store player as object: {x, y, width, height, vx, hitbox}. Input via keydown/keyup tracking (not event-per-frame). Hitbox: {x: player.x + 4, y: player.y + 6.5, w: 32, h: 51}.

userStories.title: Sedan traffic spawning and movement
userStories.description: As a player, I want sedans to appear from the top of the screen and scroll downward so that I have something to dodge.
acceptanceCriteria: Sedan: blue rectangle (#1E88E5), 40x60 px, spawns centered in a random lane
Sedan own speed = 60% of scroll speed (visual speed downward = 40% of scroll speed)
Spawns at Y=-60 (above screen), removed when Y > 760 (below screen)
Spawn interval: one sedan every 1200-1600 px of distance traveled (random within range)
Max 2 simultaneous sedans on screen
Sedans have hitbox at 80% of sprite size
Traffic stored in an array, updated and rendered each frame
Verify in browser: sedans appear from top, drift downward, disappear at bottom
userStories.notes: Track distance_traveled (accumulated scroll_speed * dt). Spawn when distance since last spawn exceeds random threshold. Same direction as player — they appear to move down because player is faster.

userStories.title: AABB collision detection with game over
userStories.description: As a player, I want to die when I hit a sedan so that the core drive-dodge-die-retry loop works.
acceptanceCriteria: AABB collision detection between player hitbox (80%) and sedan hitbox (80%)
Any collision triggers GameOver state transition
Screen shake on death: canvas offset by random ±10px, decaying over 0.5s
Game over screen shows survival time (seconds) and distance traveled (in meters: px/100)
Enter key on game over restarts: all traffic cleared, player reset, timers reset, scroll speed reset
Retry transition takes < 300ms (no loading, instant reset)
No stale state between runs (verify by playing 3+ runs consecutively)
Verify in browser: collision feels fair (80% hitbox means visual near-misses survive)
userStories.notes: AABB check: !(a.right < b.left || a.left > b.right || a.bottom < b.top || a.top > b.bottom). Reset function must zero ALL game state variables.

userStories.title: Scroll speed ramping over time
userStories.description: As a player, I want the road to get faster over time so that each run has escalating tension.
acceptanceCriteria: Scroll speed formula: min(200 + elapsed_seconds * 5, 600) for first ~80s
After 600 px/s: ramp reduces to +1.5 px/s per second
Hard cap at 800 px/s (never exceeds)
Speed displayed in debug mode (optional small text, toggle with D key)
Traffic spawn interval still works correctly at high speeds
Verify in browser: game starts slow, builds to fast, never exceeds 800
userStories.notes: Formula: if speed < 600: speed = 200 + elapsed * 5; else: speed = min(speed + 1.5 * dt, 800). This makes the game progressively harder — the core difficulty driver.

userStories.title: Full traffic system with 4 vehicle types
userStories.description: As a player, I want to face trucks, sedans, sports cars, and motorcycles with different behaviors so that dodging stays varied and interesting.
acceptanceCriteria: Truck: gray (#616161), 60x72 px, own speed 40% of scroll. Available from start
Sedan: blue (#1E88E5), 40x60 px, own speed 60% of scroll. Available from start
Sports car: dark red (#B71C1C), 36x58 px, own speed 85% of scroll. Appears after 40s. Changes to adjacent lane randomly (smooth interpolation over 0.8s, triggers every 2-4s)
Motorcycle: yellow (#FFC107), 20x48 px, own speed 70% of scroll. Appears after 60s. Weaves between lanes every 1-2s
All have 80% hitboxes
Type weighting: aggressive types 0% at 0s, 15% at 30s, 35% at 60s, 60% at 90s+
Verify in browser: each type looks distinct, sports car/moto visibly change lanes
userStories.notes: Refactor traffic from US-005 to use a type system. Each type: {color, width, height, speedRatio, behavior, minTime}. Lane change: lerp(currentX, targetLaneX, t) over 0.8s.

userStories.title: Fairness constraint for traffic spawning
userStories.description: As a designer, I need to guarantee every death is the player's fault by ensuring there's always a passable gap in traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Before spawning traffic, validate: within any vertical band of 96px (1.5x player height), there exists a horizontal corridor of at least 60px (1.5x player width) free of obstacles
Corridor check accounts for projected positions of lane-changing vehicles over next 1.0s
If constraint would be violated, delay spawn until safe
Trucks (60px wide) in adjacent lanes must be caught as blocking (60+60=120 > lane, only 80-60=20px gap per lane)
Debug mode (D key): draws free corridors as green semi-transparent overlay
Verify in browser: toggle debug mode, confirm green corridors always visible during gameplay
userStories.notes: Scan algorithm: for each Y band, project all vehicles into X ranges (including future positions for lane-changers), find largest gap. If largest gap < 60px, reject spawn. This is the game's 'fairness promise'.

userStories.title: Graded collision system (lateral vs frontal)
userStories.description: As a player, I want glancing blows to slow me down instead of killing me so that not every touch is instant death.
acceptanceCriteria: Collision classified by approach direction: if abs(player.vx) > abs(relative_vy) * 0.5 then lateral, else frontal
Lateral (glancing): 20% scroll speed reduction for 0.8s, yellow spark particles (4-8 particles, 0.2s life)
Frontal (hard hit): 40% scroll speed reduction for 1.5s, -15 fuel (only if fuel system exists, else skip), red flash overlay 20% opacity for 0.15s, screen shake 6px/0.3s
Border collision (car at road edge): 30% speed reduction for 1s
Fatal collision: instant game over when scroll_speed > 600 AND collision is frontal type
After any collision: 1.5s invulnerability window (player blinks, no further collisions)
Speed reduction is multiplicative on scroll_speed, auto-recovers after duration
Verify in browser: side-swiping a sedan = sparks + slowdown. Head-on at high speed = death
userStories.notes: relative_vy = scroll_speed - vehicle.ownSpeed (how fast vehicle approaches on screen). Invulnerability prevents chain-death spiral. Blink effect: toggle visibility every 0.1s during invuln.

userStories.title: Fuel system with drain and collection
userStories.description: As a player, I want a fuel gauge that constantly drains and can be refilled by risky pickups so that sessions have a natural time limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial fuel: 100 units
Consumption formula: 2.0 + (scroll_speed / 800) * 3.0 units/second (continuous, proportional to speed)
When slowed by collision, fuel consumption drops proportionally (hidden mercy mechanic)
Fuel item: green circle (#43A047) with subtle glow effect, 24px diameter
Fuel spawns every 900-1400 px traveled, interval increases +5px per second elapsed
Collecting fuel: +20 units at 0-59s, +18 at 60-89s, +15 at 90s+. Cap at 100
60% chance fuel spawns in lane adjacent to traffic (risky positioning)
Collection: player hitbox overlaps fuel item hitbox (100% of item size)
Item collect animation: scale to 120% then fade out over 0.2s
Game over when fuel reaches 0 (show 'Fuel Empty' as cause of death)
Verify in browser: fuel drains visibly, collecting fuel extends run, game ends when empty
userStories.notes: At 200 px/s: 2.75 u/s (~36s to empty without collecting). At 500 px/s: 3.875 u/s. At 800 px/s: 5.0 u/s. This is the session timer — even perfect play eventually runs out.

userStories.title: Fuel bar HUD element
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my fuel level at a glance so I know when to take risks for refills.
acceptanceCriteria: Fuel bar at top of screen, full canvas width (400px), 4px tall
Color gradient based on fuel level: green (#43A047) above 50%, yellow (#FFC107) 20-50%, red (#E53935) below 20%
Bar width proportional to fuel (100 units = full width)
Bar flashes (blink on/off every 0.3s) when fuel below 20%
Red vignette effect at screen edges when fuel < 20% (subtle dark-to-red gradient at corners)
Verify in browser: fuel bar is visible, color changes are clear, flashing is noticeable but not annoying
userStories.notes: Render on top of everything. Vignette: draw radial gradient from transparent center to rgba(255,0,0,0.15) at corners.

userStories.title: Distance scoring and HUD
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my score increase as I drive so that I feel progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Distance score: 1 point per 10 px traveled (accumulated continuously)
Clean overtake bonus: +25 points when a traffic vehicle exits bottom of screen and player never collided with it
Survival bonus: +300 points every 30 seconds without collision, brief 'SURVIVOR +300' text flash
Score displayed at top-right: large white text (#FFFFFF) with subtle drop shadow
Score counter animates smoothly (lerps to target value, no jumps)
Best score persisted in localStorage key 'roadrush_best_score'
Game over screen shows: final score, best score (golden highlight if new best), survival time, cause of death
Verify in browser: score increases during gameplay, best score persists across page reloads
userStories.notes: Use displayed_score that lerps toward actual_score for smooth counter. Overtake tracking: tag each vehicle as 'collided' boolean, check on removal.

userStories.title: Difficulty scaling (multi-axis ramp)
userStories.description: As a player, I want difficulty to escalate across multiple dimensions so that each run has a dramatic arc with increasing tension.
acceptanceCriteria: All scaling driven by elapsed_seconds as single source of truth
Scroll speed: already implemented in US-007
Traffic density: already implemented in US-008
Fuel scarcity: spawn interval grows, pickup amount shrinks, consumption rises (US-011)
After collision: 1.5s local spawn pause (no new traffic spawns within 200px above/below player Y)
Death spiral mitigation: collision slowdown reduces fuel consumption proportionally (already in US-011)
All difficulty parameters accessible from a single config/constants object for tuning
Verify in browser: play a full 90s+ run, confirm difficulty ramp feels like 4 acts (easy start → engagement → crisis → climax)
userStories.notes: This story is about wiring together existing systems and tuning. Create a DifficultyManager that reads elapsed_seconds and exposes current values for all axes. Spawn pause after collision is the key new mechanic here.

userStories.title: Near miss detection and combo system
userStories.description: As a player, I want to earn bonus points for narrowly dodging traffic and chain near misses into combos so that risky play is rewarded.
acceptanceCriteria: Track minimum horizontal distance (sprite edge to sprite edge) between player and each vehicle during the entire vertical overlap period
When vehicle's sprite bottom passes below player's sprite top (fully overtaken): if min distance was < 20px AND no collision occurred, trigger near miss
One near miss per vehicle maximum (tracked per vehicle instance ID)
Near miss awards: 50 * combo_multiplier points
Combo multiplier starts at 1, +1 for each consecutive near miss
Combo resets to 0 on collision OR 3 seconds without a near miss
Combo counter at center-top: shows 'xN' only when combo >= 2, scale-in animation, fades 3s after last near miss
Brief white flash on the near-missed vehicle's side closest to player
Verify in browser: drive close to traffic, see near miss triggers, combo counter appears at x2+
userStories.notes: Per-vehicle tracking: add minDistX and nearMissChecked to each vehicle object. Measure during vertical overlap (vehicle.bottom > player.top && vehicle.top < player.bottom). Check on exit.

userStories.title: Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment (DDA)
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the game to subtly pressure skilled players while respecting fairness so that every run eventually ends.
acceptanceCriteria: Every 10 seconds without collision: +5% traffic spawn rate (multiplicative)
Active near miss combo >= 3: enemies spawn 10% closer together
Fuel above 70%: next fuel spawns 20% further away
DDA cap: maximum +50% total adjustment to any single parameter
DDA partial reset on collision: reduce accumulated DDA bonuses by 50%
DDA fully resets on game over
DDA adjustments must respect fairness constraint (US-009) — fairness always wins
Debug mode (D key): show DDA multiplier values as text overlay
Verify in browser: toggle debug, confirm DDA values increase during clean play, partially reset on hit
userStories.notes: DDA is the 'invisible hand' that ensures skilled players still die. Cap prevents it from creating impossible situations. Partial reset on collision gives breathing room without fully removing pressure.

userStories.title: Coin collectibles
userStories.description: As a player, I want to collect coins for bonus points so that there are more micro-decisions during a run.
acceptanceCriteria: Coin: golden circle (#FFD700), 16px diameter
Coins spawn in clusters of 3-5 in an arc/line pattern every 600-1000 px traveled
Each coin awards +100 points on collection
Collection: player hitbox (80%) overlaps coin hitbox (100%)
Collect animation: scale to 120% then fade out 0.2s, '+100' text floats upward and fades
Coins scroll downward with the road (same as traffic relative movement)
Verify in browser: coin clusters appear regularly, collecting feels satisfying with popup
userStories.notes: Cluster patterns: line (same X, spaced 40px Y), arc (slight X offset per coin), zigzag. Pick randomly. Coins are low-risk reward — they don't need to be in dangerous positions.

userStories.title: Nitro boost item
userStories.description: As a player, I want to collect a rare nitro power-up that gives a temporary speed boost and invulnerability.
acceptanceCriteria: Nitro item: yellow triangle (#FFD600), 20px, pulsing scale animation (0.9x to 1.1x, 0.5s cycle)
Rare spawn: every 2500-4000 px traveled
On collect: +30% scroll speed for 3 seconds + full invulnerability during boost
Blue particle trail behind car during nitro (4-6 particles per frame, 0.3s life, drift downward)
Speed returns to normal smoothly after 3s (ease-out over 0.5s)
Collect animation: scale to 120% then fade out 0.2s
Verify in browser: nitro is visually distinct, boost feels powerful, particles trail behind car
userStories.notes: During nitro, skip collision checks entirely (invulnerable). Particles: spawn at car bottom-center, random spread ±10px X, velocity downward 50-100 px/s, color #42A5F5 fading to transparent.

userStories.title: Shield item
userStories.description: As a player, I want a very rare shield that absorbs one hit so that I get a second chance in critical moments.
acceptanceCriteria: Shield item: blue hexagon (#42A5F5), 22px
Very rare spawn: every 5000+ px traveled
On collect: shield becomes active (stored as boolean on player state)
Active shield visual: subtle blue glow/border around player car sprite
Shield absorbs exactly 1 collision of any type (including what would be fatal), then disappears
When shield absorbs: flash the shield border brightly then fade out over 0.3s
Only 1 shield can be held at a time (collecting another while active does nothing)
Verify in browser: shield glows on car, absorbs a collision visually, then disappears
userStories.notes: In collision handler: if player.hasShield, set hasShield=false, play shield-break VFX, skip all collision effects. Simple and impactful.

userStories.title: Speed lines and final VFX polish
userStories.description: As a player, I want visual effects that communicate speed and impact so that the game feels juicy without audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Speed lines: white semi-transparent vertical lines on left/right road margins when scroll speed > 500 px/s
More lines and faster movement at higher speeds (scale count and speed with scroll_speed)
Spark particles on lateral collision: 4-8 yellow/orange particles with random velocity, 0.2s life
Red flash overlay on frontal collision: full-screen red at 20% opacity, fades over 0.15s
Screen shake respects budget: lateral 2px/0.15s, frontal 6px/0.3s, fatal 10px/0.5s (exponential decay)
Explosion on fatal death: 3 concentric circles expanding + fading over 0.5s (white to transparent)
All VFX use a shared particle/effect manager for clean update/render lifecycle
Verify in browser: speed lines appear at high speed, collisions have visible feedback, death explosion is satisfying
userStories.notes: Particle system: array of {x, y, vx, vy, life, maxLife, color, size}. Update: move by velocity, reduce life. Render: draw circle with alpha = life/maxLife. Remove when life <= 0.
